title: 951. How many classical dance forms are officially recognized in India?
hint: The number is recognized by the Sangeet Natak Akademi.
summary: India has eight officially recognized classical dance forms.

title: 952. Bharatanatyam originated in which state?
hint: It developed from temple traditions of southern India.
summary: Bharatanatyam originated in Tamil Nadu.

title: 953. Kathak is mainly associated with which region of India?
hint: It developed in northern India.
summary: Kathak is a classical dance form of North India.

title: 954. Kuchipudi originated from which state?
hint: It is named after a village in southern India.
summary: Kuchipudi originated in Andhra Pradesh.

title: 955. Odissi is the classical dance form of which state?
hint: It developed around temple traditions in eastern India.
summary: Odissi originated in Odisha.

title: 956. Kathakali originated in which state?
hint: It is famous for elaborate makeup and costumes.
summary: Kathakali originated in Kerala.

title: 957. Manipuri classical dance belongs to which state?
hint: It comes from Northeast India.
summary: Manipuri originated in Manipur.

title: 958. Sattriya classical dance originated in which state?
hint: It developed in Vaishnav monasteries.
summary: Sattriya originated in Assam.

title: 959. Mohiniyattam is a classical dance form of which state?
hint: The name means the dance of an enchantress.
summary: Mohiniyattam originated in Kerala.

title: 960. Which text is considered the foundation of Indian classical dance theory?
hint: It is an ancient Sanskrit text on performing arts.
summary: Natya Shastra by Bharata Muni is the foundation of Indian classical dance theory.

title: 961. Which classical dance form is known for its fast footwork and spins?
hint: It developed in the courts of North India.
summary: Kathak is known for fast footwork and spins.

title: 962. Which classical dance form uses the tribhanga posture?
hint: It originated in eastern India.
summary: Odissi is known for the tribhanga posture.

title: 963. Which classical dance form is famous for expressive eye movements and facial expressions?
hint: It is a dance-drama form from Kerala.
summary: Kathakali is famous for facial expressions and eye movements.

title: 964. Which classical dance form was revived by Rukmini Devi Arundale?
hint: It is one of the oldest classical dances of India.
summary: Rukmini Devi Arundale played an important role in reviving Bharatanatyam.

title: 965. Which musical instrument is commonly associated with Kathak?
hint: It is a percussion instrument used in Hindustani music.
summary: Tabla is commonly associated with Kathak performances.

title: 966. Which classical dance form uses the drum called Pung?
hint: It belongs to Northeast India.
summary: Manipuri dance uses the Pung drum.

title: 967. Who is known as the father of Kathak?
hint: He was a legendary dancer from North India.
summary: Pandit Birju Maharaj is considered one of the greatest Kathak dancers.

title: 968. Kelucharan Mohapatra was associated with which classical dance?
hint: He was a legendary guru from eastern India.
summary: Kelucharan Mohapatra was a famous Odissi dancer.

title: 969. Which classical dance form is known for storytelling through hand gestures called mudras?
hint: Most Indian classical dances use this technique.
summary: Bharatanatyam is especially known for expressive mudras.

title: 970. Which classical dance form is traditionally performed by male artists called Chakyars?
hint: It is a temple art form of Kerala.
summary: Koodiyattam is associated with Chakyar performers.

title: 971. Which classical dance form uses the costume with a fan-shaped pleated design in front?
hint: It is a famous dance form from Tamil Nadu.
summary: Bharatanatyam costume is known for its fan-shaped pleats.

title: 972. Which classical dance form is performed with elaborate facial makeup called Aharya Abhinaya?
hint: It is famous as a dance-drama tradition of Kerala.
summary: Kathakali is known for elaborate makeup and costumes.

title: 973. The word 'Kathak' is derived from which Sanskrit word?
hint: The meaning is related to storytelling.
summary: Kathak comes from the Sanskrit word Katha meaning story.

title: 974. Which classical dance form has the famous Lucknow and Jaipur gharanas?
hint: It belongs to northern India.
summary: Kathak has famous Lucknow and Jaipur gharanas.

title: 975. Which instrument is commonly used in Bharatanatyam performances?
hint: It is a percussion instrument from South India.
summary: Mridangam is commonly used in Bharatanatyam.

title: 976. Which classical dance form includes the character of Krishna prominently?
hint: It is associated with Vaishnav traditions.
summary: Manipuri dance often depicts stories of Krishna.

title: 977. Who is known as the father of modern Odissi?
hint: He was a famous guru from Odisha.
summary: Kelucharan Mohapatra is regarded as a major figure in Odissi revival.

title: 978. Which classical dance form originated from Andhra Pradesh and combines dance with drama?
hint: It is named after a village.
summary: Kuchipudi combines dance and dramatic performance.

title: 979. Which classical dance form is performed with graceful and gentle movements?
hint: It belongs to Kerala and is associated with feminine grace.
summary: Mohiniyattam is known for graceful movements.

title: 980. Which classical dance form was added to the list of recognized classical dances in 2000?
hint: It belongs to Northeast India.
summary: Sattriya was recognized as a classical dance form in 2000.

title: 981. Who wrote the ancient treatise on Indian performing arts?
hint: It is considered the foundation of classical dance theory.
summary: Bharata Muni wrote the Natya Shastra.

title: 982. Which classical dance form is based on the stories of Hindu epics and mythology?
hint: It is a famous dance-drama from Kerala.
summary: Kathakali commonly presents stories from epics and mythology.

title: 983. Rukmini Devi Arundale founded which institution for dance education?
hint: It is located in Chennai.
summary: Rukmini Devi founded Kalakshetra Foundation.

title: 984. Which classical dance form uses the costume called 'Panchakachcham'?
hint: It is associated with Tamil Nadu.
summary: Panchakachcham style costume is associated with Bharatanatyam.

title: 985. Which classical dance form is associated with the Vaishnavite monasteries called Sattras?
hint: It belongs to Northeast India.
summary: Sattriya developed in Assam's Sattras.

title: 986. Who among the following is a famous exponent of Bharatanatyam?
hint: She played an important role in revival of the dance form.
summary: Rukmini Devi Arundale was a famous Bharatanatyam exponent.

title: 987. Which institution is responsible for promoting music, dance and drama in India?
hint: It is India's national academy for performing arts.
summary: Sangeet Natak Akademi promotes performing arts in India.

title: 988. Which classical dance form uses the Ras Leela theme?
hint: It is associated with Krishna devotion.
summary: Manipuri commonly depicts Ras Leela.

title: 989. Which classical dance form is also called the dance of the enchantress?
hint: It originated in Kerala.
summary: Mohiniyattam means dance of the enchantress.

title: 990. Which classical dance form was influenced by the Vaishnav Bhakti movement in Assam?
hint: It was developed by Srimanta Sankardeva.
summary: Sattriya was developed by Srimanta Sankardeva.

title: 991. Which classical dance form is performed in the traditional white and gold costume?
hint: It is associated with Kerala.
summary: Mohiniyattam is known for white and gold costumes.

title: 992. Which Indian classical dance form has strong influences from temple sculptures?
hint: It developed in the temples of eastern India.
summary: Odissi has strong connections with temple sculptures and traditions.

title: 993. Which classical dance form is known for the use of circular movements and gentle expressions?
hint: It belongs to Northeast India.
summary: Manipuri is known for graceful circular movements.

title: 994. Which classical dance form is associated with the Jagannath Temple tradition?
hint: It originated in Odisha.
summary: Odissi developed from traditions linked with Odisha temples.

title: 995. Which organization recognizes Indian classical dance forms at the national level?
hint: It is an academy under the Ministry of Culture.
summary: Sangeet Natak Akademi recognizes classical dance forms.

title: 996. Which Indian classical dance form was added to UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list as part of related traditions?
hint: It is connected with Kerala's traditional theatre.
summary: Koodiyattam is recognized by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage tradition.

title: 997. Which dance form combines martial art movements with performance?
hint: It is a traditional dance-drama from Kerala.
summary: Kathakali includes elements of physical training and martial traditions.

title: 998. Which famous dancer is associated with the revival of Kathakali?
hint: He was a famous Kerala dancer and guru.
summary: Vallathol Narayana Menon contributed greatly to Kathakali revival.

title: 999. Which classical dance form uses the drum instrument called Maddalam?
hint: It is associated with Kerala.
summary: Maddalam is used in Kathakali performances.

title: 1000. Which ancient concept describes the emotional experience created in Indian performing arts?
hint: It is one of the key concepts in Natya Shastra.
summary: Rasa describes the emotional essence experienced by the audience.
